seo.title: Context Window Management for AI Agents in Google ADK Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix context window management for ai agents in Google ADK agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Google ADK, Context Window Management issues for AI agents arise from multi-agent delegation exceeding Gemini's token limits, causing truncated histories and failed handoffs. Session state accumulation in long conversations corrupts agent memory, while tool response mismatches inflate context unnecessarily. These problems manifest uniquely through ADK's agent orchestration patterns and Gemini model constraints.

howItManifests.symptom: Agent history exceeds 1M token limit during handoffs between DelegateAgent and WorkerAgent
howItManifests.impact: Gemini returns 'context too long' errors, breaking conversation flow

howItManifests.symptom: Context window size mismatch causes sudden truncation mid-conversation
howItManifests.impact: Agents lose critical session state, producing incoherent responses

howItManifests.symptom: ADK's default SessionState accumulates every message indefinitely
howItManifests.impact: Performance degradation → complete failure after 20-30 turns

howItManifests.symptom: Agents return verbose JSON instead of structured ToolResponse objects
howItManifests.impact: Context balloons 5-10x, hitting limits in 5-7 interactions

detection.name: Token Limit Monitor
detection.description: Tracks context window usage before each agent invocation using ADK's message history

detection.name: Session State Bloat Detector
detection.description: Analyzes ADK SessionState growth rate across conversation turns

detection.name: Tool Response Size Analyzer
detection.description: Measures tool response verbosity across agent interactions

fixes.name: Dynamic Context Pruning
fixes.description: Automatically trims oldest messages before hitting Gemini limits

fixes.name: State Compression Middleware
fixes.description: Compresses SessionState using JSON delta encoding

fixes.name: Structured Tool Responses
fixes.description: Enforces compact ToolResponse format across all agents

vexAutomation.heading: Automating Context Window Management for AI Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates as a before_agent_callback in Google ADK, automatically monitoring context window usage, session state growth, and tool response bloat across all agent interactions. It applies intelligent pruning and compression in real-time, preventing failures before they occur. Configuration is zero-code via Vex dashboard policies.

Vex's ADK-specific detectors track Gemini token limits per model variant (1.5 Pro/Flash, 2.0), correlate multi-agent delegation patterns, and enforce response formatting standards. Remediation happens automatically with fallback behaviors like context summarization and state eviction.

The platform provides observability dashboards showing context usage trends, failure patterns by agent, and ROI from prevented outages.
vexAutomation.code:
from vex_sdk import before_agent_callback
from google.adk.agents import Agent

# Single line integration
app = before_agent_callback(
    detectors=['context_window', 'session_bloat', 'tool_response'],
    remediation=['auto_prune', 'state_compress'],
    alert_on='context_overflow'
)

# All ADK agents automatically protected
planner_agent = PlannerAgent()
worker_agent = WorkerAgent()

# Vex handles everything transparently
response = planner_agent.invoke(messages)

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Install with `pip install google-adk vex-sdk` and add one line to protect all your ADK agents from context window failures.
